summary: A stellar audience recording that captures a superb Other One, but the overall set feels work‑manlike and unremarkable.
review: The tape is praised as an “AUD” of the highest caliber – crisp, lively and on par with many soundboard releases. Musically the night shines when Bobby Weir erupts on The Other One, yet several listeners find the rest of the early‑80s setlist flat and not worth repeated listening.

ai_rating.rationale: Excellent recording quality (30% weight) combined with a mixed musical impression – a standout Other One but generally lukewarm set – yields an overall solid rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crystal‑clear, comparable to soundboard tapes; praised for its live ambience and lack of muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑80s material comes across as work‑manlike; one reviewer finds the setlist uninspired after a strong opening with "Stranger".

band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for an unleashed performance on The Other One.
